messages.content:
Combine below points into a paragraph.
	Panel leadership input – facilitation/coaching/training/ensuring non-direct reports (RXs/PMs) are engaged & motivated = quality outputs. 
	Training new staff – Sydney for leadership/facilitation/sample planning/ logistics/networking/interaction skills, liaising with the RXs/lab staffs re: session/sample plans as per Sensory SOPs.
	Training/mentoring less experienced staff in technical work – Aimee with line scales, panel setup, sample logistics and mentoring with peers. 
	Provided presentation/guidance to FGTPs from 2020-2022. Collaborate with the FGTPS- Bianca in DFC assessments, covering methodology/logistic, using blind control, implementing safety, and data analysis. Also, demonstrated/trained Clarissa on panel process for protein bar matrix validation.
	Organise/schedule Monthly Meetings between Technical Team and RXs – to aid in development of team strategy. 
	Trainer for Minitab to newer staff on doing interval plots/ main effects/ scree plots/ bar charts and scatterplots for storage trial – including Erin and Alice in Shanghai.
	Demonstrator on R studio to newer staff for doing projective napping, followed by MAF analysis through XLSTATS. 
	Trainer for Compusense – tools for screening/recruitment.
	Mentoring laboratory staff to build expertise – clear explanation of project objectives and therefore required techniques/SOPs (HOT blocks, Pizza cutting and thermomixer validation)
	Collaborating with Emily F with her Focus Group on creamy texture with the Chinese Population in New Zealand. Helping with sample prep and translation from English to Chinese- which include instructions for each exercise section and all the discussions/ conversations between the Chinese panellists.

In my role, I provide leadership input to the panel, facilitating and coaching them to ensure engagement and motivation, resulting in high-quality outputs. I also train new staff in various areas, including leadership, facilitation, sample planning, logistics, networking, and interaction skills. Additionally, I liaise with RXs and lab staff to ensure session and sample plans are in accordance with Sensory SOPs. I mentor less experienced staff in technical work, such as line scales, panel setup, sample logistics, and provide mentoring with peers.

I have also had the opportunity to provide presentations and guidance to FGTPs from 2020-2022. I collaborate with the FGTPS, particularly with Bianca in DFC assessments, covering methodology, logistics, blind control, safety implementation, and data analysis. I have also demonstrated and trained Clarissa on the panel process for protein bar matrix validation.

Furthermore, I organize and schedule monthly meetings between the Technical Team and RXs to aid in the development of team strategy. As a trainer, I have taught newer staff Minitab, specifically interval plots, main effects, scree plots, bar charts, and scatterplots for storage trials. This training has included Erin and Alice in Shanghai. Additionally, I am a demonstrator on R Studio, training newer staff on projective napping and MAF analysis through XLSTATS.

In terms of tools, I am a trainer for Compusense, providing training on screening and recruitment. I also mentor laboratory staff, providing clear explanations of project objectives and the required techniques and SOPs, such as HOT blocks, pizza cutting, and thermomixer validation.

Lastly, I have collaborated with Emily F on her Focus Group, specifically on creamy texture with the Chinese population in New Zealand. I have assisted with sample preparation and translation from English to Chinese, including instructions for each exercise section and facilitating discussions between the Chinese panellists.

Combine below points into a paragraph and very condensed.
 Panel leadership input – facilitation/coaching/training/ensuring non-direct reports (RXs/PMs) are engaged & motivated = quality outputs.
 Training new staff – Sydney for leadership/facilitation/sample planning/ logistics/networking/interaction skills, liaising with the RXs/lab staffs re: session/sample plans as per Sensory SOPs.
 Training/mentoring less experienced staff in technical work – Aimee with line scales, panel setup, sample logistics and mentoring with peers.
 Provided presentation/guidance to FGTPs from 2020-2022. Collaborate with the FGTPS- Bianca in DFC assessments, covering methodology/logistic, using blind control, implementing safety, and data analysis. Also, demonstrated/trained Clarissa on panel process for protein bar matrix validation.
 Organise/schedule Monthly Meetings between Technical Team and RXs – to aid in development of team strategy.
 Trainer for Minitab to newer staff on doing interval plots/ main effects/ scree plots/ bar charts and scatterplots for storage trial – including Erin and Alice in Shanghai.
 Demonstrator on R studio to newer staff for doing projective napping, followed by MAF analysis through XLSTATS.
 Trainer for Compusense – tools for screening/recruitment.
 Mentoring laboratory staff to build expertise – clear explanation of project objectives and therefore required techniques/SOPs (HOT blocks, Pizza cutting and thermomixer validation)
 Collaborating with Emily F with her Focus Group on creamy texture with the Chinese Population in New Zealand. Helping with sample prep and translation from English to Chinese- which include instructions for each exercise section and all the discussions/ conversations between the Chinese panellists.

In my role, I provide panel leadership input and training to ensure non-direct reports are motivated and produce quality outputs. I train new staff in leadership, facilitation, sample planning, logistics, networking, and interaction skills. Additionally, I mentor less experienced staff in technical work, collaborate with FGTPs and Bianca in DFC assessments, organize monthly meetings between the Technical Team and RXs, and provide training on Minitab and R Studio. I also serve as a trainer for Compusense and mentor laboratory staff, while collaborating with Emily F on a Focus Group project involving sample preparation and translation.

Combine below points into a paragraph and very condensed.
	To ensure alignment between the Product Experience Sciences at FRDC and the Consumer Science department in Shanghai, regularly facilitate meetings with Erin S to align the NZ and Shanghai departments in sensory best practices. With focus on implementing FRDC standards, especially in the Gen 2 project. Been collaborated with Erin and Jasmine through virtual training, including an orientation training and three virtual tasting sessions, to improve evaluation quality and project outcomes.
	Gen 2 – led showcase tasting for Chinese NZMP guests, explained bi-polar CTC method, and guided/directed sensory best practices, encouraged feedback in Mandarin/English, facilitated team discussion bi-lingually.
	Pacific – Continues to lead the external team training, introduced improved references, and detailed matrix, and presented to the Hawkins Watts team. Actively collaborated on slice and burger matrix alignment and evolution.

I regularly facilitate meetings with Erin S to align sensory best practices between the Product Experience Sciences at FRDC and the Consumer Science department in Shanghai, ensuring implementation of FRDC standards. I have collaborated with Erin and Jasmine through virtual training, including an orientation training and three virtual tasting sessions, to enhance evaluation quality and project outcomes. Additionally, I led a showcase tasting for Chinese NZMP guests in the Gen 2 project, explaining the bi-polar CTC method and guiding sensory best practices while facilitating bilingual team discussions. Furthermore, I continue to lead external team training for the Pacific project, introducing improved references and matrix while collaborating on slice and burger alignment and evolution with the Hawkins Watts team.

Combine below points into a paragraph and very condensed.
	Led panellist recruitment in September 2022 and June 2023 with Maddy. Updated “Who are We” PowerPoint, conducted screeners' introduction, organised sessions, gathered results timely, and led final selection of candidates’ discussions with sensory technical team.
	Pacific – Continue to liaise with Ivan, Eamonn, and Jane re: team tastings and visitor's showcase. Held pre-session meetings to address any questions/logistic concerns on a regular basis to ensure accuracy.
	Consistently participated in Gen 2 and Pacific project teams' meetings, contributing/supporting in Sprint reviews and weekly technical meetings. Delivered comprehensive presentations with sensory insights/themes and updates, ensuring seamless collaboration and alignment among team members.

With Maddy's assistance, I led panellist recruitment in September 2022 and June 2023, updating the "Who are We" PowerPoint, conducting screeners' introductions, organizing sessions, gathering results promptly, and leading final selection discussions with the sensory technical team. In the Pacific project, I maintained regular communication with Ivan, Eamonn, and Jane, addressing questions and logistics through pre-session meetings to ensure accuracy. Additionally, I actively participated in meetings for both the Gen 2 and Pacific projects, contributing to sprint reviews and weekly technical meetings, and delivering comprehensive presentations with sensory insights and updates to foster seamless collaboration and alignment among team members.

Combine below points into a paragraph and very condensed.
	Incorporated the COVID SOPs (written up by Deanne Kusabs) with proper distancing in the critical project “Dairy Modelling” #107535. Supported to prep/run panel during pandemic with decreased staffs, also, recommended logistics/proper distancing and SOPs were followed as per food and safety requirements. 
	Collaborated with the Shanghai Consumer Science Team, including Nicki F, Nathalie J, and regular catchups with Erin S. Sent New Zealand references to Eurofin Shanghai for evaluation and identified alternative options in the Chinese market. Proactively coordinated with flavour houses in Shanghai and New Zealand for alignment on cream product references.

In the critical project "Dairy Modelling" #107535, I incorporated the COVID SOPs written by Deanne Kusabs to ensure proper distancing and adherence to food and safety requirements. Despite decreased staff, I supported the preparation and running of the panel during the pandemic, recommending logistics and proper distancing measures. I also collaborated with the Shanghai Consumer Science Team, including Nicki F and Nathalie J, and regularly communicated with Erin S. Additionally, I sent New Zealand references to Eurofin Shanghai for evaluation and coordinated with flavour houses in Shanghai and New Zealand to align on cream product references, identifying alternative options in the Chinese market.

Combine below points into a paragraph and very condensed.
	Make it happen with Robyn T for the Mako Mako road flooring project. Strategised contingency plans in meetings with Buddhi S. As well as engaged Justin B from food assurance for realistic sensory plans, although sessions were unnecessary due to smooth production. Our proactive preparation proved valuable in handling potential challenges within a limited timeframe.
	Demonstrated strong leadership by efficiently coordinating a discussion with three RXs to prioritise schedules and projects. Suggested solutions that accommodated everyone's needs without excessive compromise, highlighting the ability to work neutrally and listen attentively which exemplifies Fonterra's values.
	Collaborated with Anne on a Cream Profiling project. Managed panel sessions with emphasis on precautions with biohazard samples. Implemented measures like double bagging with correct green card numbers. Utilised projective napping with duplicated samples for data collection. Achieved high satisfaction/smooth planning from Anne and project team, demonstrated by closely clustered duplicates on the map.

I worked with Robyn T on the Mako Mako road flooring project, strategizing contingency plans with Buddhi S and engaging <PRESIDIO_ANONYMIZED_PERSON> for realistic sensory plans. Although sessions were ultimately unnecessary due to smooth production, our proactive preparation proved valuable in handling potential challenges within a limited timeframe. Additionally, I efficiently coordinated a discussion with three RXs to prioritize schedules and projects, suggesting solutions that accommodated everyone's needs without excessive compromise, exemplifying Fonterra's values of neutrality and attentive listening. Moreover, I collaborated with Anne on a Cream Profiling project, managing panel sessions with precautions for biohazard samples and implementing measures such as double bagging with correct green card numbers. Utilizing projective napping with duplicated samples for data collection, we achieved high satisfaction and smooth planning, as demonstrated by closely clustered duplicates on the map, earning praise from Anne and the project team.

Combine below points into a paragraph and very condensed.
	Collaborated with Joenalyn regarding the PIM/PID template in line with the business strategy. In my role as a collator, I approached RXs and gathered/organised templates from them. I actively facilitated communication between lab staff, sensory technologists, and researchers to address various needs for ongoing improvements based on valuable feedback and suggestions.
	Utilises awareness of “working style” with implementing “the myers briggs mbti personality test (MBTI)” from Emma’s workshop - to customise communications accordingly, e.g., providing “heads up” and “regular catch ups” to Anne via Teams/What’s App vs “1:1 detailed discussion” and “plan on paper” to Robyn.
	Collaborate with Jasmin Si, Rach Guo, and Winnie Cao, as well as the Shanghai Consumer Science Team, for Gen2 team tastings. This involves regular bilingual communication, guiding sample planning, and conducting virtual tastings with proper sensory practices. Additionally, analyse and interpret results as needed.

In collaboration with Joenalyn, I worked on the PIM/PID template in alignment with the business strategy. As a collator, I gathered and organized templates from RXs, actively facilitating communication between lab staff, sensory technologists, and researchers to incorporate valuable feedback and suggestions for ongoing improvements. Additionally, I utilized awareness of working styles through the implementation of the Myers-Briggs MBTI personality test from Emma's workshop. This allowed me to customize communications accordingly, providing "heads up" and "regular catch-ups" to Anne through Teams/WhatsApp, while engaging in "1:1 detailed discussions" and "planning on paper" with Robyn. I also collaborated with Jasmin Si, Rach Guo, Winnie Cao, and the Shanghai Consumer Science Team for Gen2 team tastings. This involved regular bilingual communication, guiding sample planning, conducting virtual tastings with proper sensory practices, and analyzing and interpreting results as needed.

I collaborated with Joenalyn to develop the PIM/PID template in line with the business strategy. As a collator, I gathered and organized templates from RXs and facilitated communication between lab staff, sensory technologists, and researchers to incorporate valuable feedback for ongoing improvements. I also used the Myers-Briggs MBTI personality test to customize communications, providing tailored approaches to Anne and Robyn. Additionally, I collaborated with Jasmin Si, Rach Guo, Winnie Cao, and the Shanghai Consumer Science Team for Gen2 team tastings, conducting virtual sessions and analyzing results.

Combine below points into a paragraph and very condensed.
	Managed to clarify Erin's unclear approaches regarding sensory queries and reference questions. By involving Nicki, an SME in Cream, and consulting with our researchers, we ensured precise and accurate technical support was delivered. This was crucial for aligning Cream's attributes between the PXS Team in NZ and CS in Shanghai for the RCB Project. Additionally, I bridged the gap in sensory terminology between Erin and Alice, demonstrating a collaborative spirit and pushing the boundaries in bilingual communication within sensory science.
	During the Kaupapa Te Pou Event at FRDC, I partnered with Tim C to facilitate Katz Maihi's visit, aiming to establish a bicultural foundation that bolsters our multicultural workforce. Together, we introduced crucial concepts such as sensory evaluation, the product discovery process, and the core mission of the CS team to the broader business community.

I clarified Erin's sensory queries and reference questions by involving Nicki and consulting with our researchers to ensure precise technical support. This was crucial for aligning Cream's attributes between the PXS Team in NZ and CS in Shanghai for the RCB Project. I also bridged the gap in sensory terminology between Erin and Alice, demonstrating a collaborative spirit in bilingual communication within sensory science. Additionally, during the Kaupapa Te Pou Event at FRDC, I partnered with Tim C to facilitate Katz Maihi's visit, establishing a bicultural foundation and introducing important concepts to the broader business community.

Combine below points into a paragraph and very condensed.
	Core requirement as Sensory Technologist training/calibrating panellists as evidenced by successful technical support to projects/company, e.g. Gen 2- trained/led informal panel, provided guidance.
	Involvement in the future talent project – Puhoro Event. Prompted the students for the Compusense exercise and interpreted/adjusted data presentation while showing them the results." 
	Displayed exceptional communication skills as panel leads when training new Sensory Technologists (Sydney/ Aimee). Provided guidance on the informal tasting procedure to FGTPS (Bianca and Clarissa) with accurate information/data presented.
	Clear demonstration when leading/training others to lead informal “Project Team tastings (Pacific/ Gen 2/ Cultured Team) and especially the sensory training workshops in both Japan and China alongside Maddy P. and Nicki F. This involved maintaining open lines of communication through Teams and emails, tailoring training based on business priorities, and applying the training model and matrix developed during the Indo training. We customised the training process/procedures to suit specific environments as needed.

As a Sensory Technologist, I have successfully trained and calibrated panellists for various projects, such as Gen 2, providing guidance and support. I also played a role in the future talent project, specifically during the Puhoro Event, where I prompted students for the Compusense exercise and interpreted and adjusted data presentations. In training new Sensory Technologists and leading informal tastings, I displayed exceptional communication skills and provided accurate information. Additionally, I demonstrated clear leadership in leading informal project team tastings for the Pacific, Gen 2, and Cultured teams, including sensory training workshops in Japan and China. This involved maintaining open communication, tailoring training based on business priorities, and customizing the training process to suit specific environments.
